labs.clinicalContext: High hypoglycemia risk with sulfonylureas, especially in CKD patients. Risk increases when combined with other glucose-lowering agents
Long-acting or renally-cleared sulfonylureas should be avoided at low eGFRs due to increased hypoglycemia risk

labRequirements.specialPopulationNotes: Sulfonylureas that are long-acting or cleared by the kidney should be avoided at low eGFRs. Higher HbA1c target (e.g., <7.5% or <8%) may be selected for patients at higher risk for hypoglycemia treated with sulfonylureas.
labRequirements.sourceSnippet: Sulfonylureas that are long-acting or cleared by the kidney should be avoided at low eGFRs. Figure 23: Sulfonylurea — Discontinue when eGFR < 30; Dialysis: Discontinue. Risk of hypoglycemia is high in patients with advanced CKD who are treated by glucose-lowering agents that raise blood insulin levels (sulfonylureas).

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease
